headline: Bills Fans Get First Look at New Highmark Stadium
writeup: The biggest story surrounding the Buffalo Bills on July 5, 2026 is the team's announcement of an open practice on August 18 at the brand-new Highmark Stadium, giving fans — including those without season tickets — a rare early glimpse of the franchise's gleaming new home. As training camp approaches, roster battles are heating up across multiple position groups, with linebackers and offensive linemen drawing particular scrutiny over who will lock down spots versus who remains on the bubble. On the offensive side, anticipation is building around newly acquired wide receiver DJ Moore, with NFL insiders hinting at a breakout debut season in coordinator Joe Brady's system. Defensively, first-year coordinator Jim Leonhard is under the microscope, with ESPN labeling his job one of the toughest in the NFL in 2026, while safety CJ Gardner-Johnson looks to silence critics who questioned his locker room presence. Off the field, ex-Bills star Marcellus Wiley made headlines for the wrong reasons, while former coach Sean McDermott is already being floated as a head coaching candidate by PFF.
